title: Ordering at a Restaurant
emotional_stakes: You're very hungry and the waiter is coming to your table right now.
ai_opening: Good evening! Welcome to The Golden Plate. Here's our menu. Are you ready to order, or would you like a few more minutes?
learning_goal: Practice ordering food and making polite requests

title: Checking In at a Hotel
emotional_stakes: You just arrived after a long 12-hour flight and you're exhausted.
ai_opening: Welcome to the Sunrise Hotel! Do you have a reservation with us?
learning_goal: Practice hotel check-in conversation and travel vocabulary

title: Job Interview
emotional_stakes: You really want this job. The interviewer looks serious but friendly.
ai_opening: Hello! I'm the hiring manager at TechCorp. Thank you for coming in today. Could you start by telling me a little about yourself?
learning_goal: Practice formal self-introduction and work vocabulary
rescue_hint: You could start with: I have worked in... for... years

title: Making New Friends at a Party
emotional_stakes: You don't know anyone here. Someone friendly is walking towards you.
ai_opening: Hey! I don't think we've met before. I'm Alex. Are you a friend of the host?
learning_goal: Practice casual introductions and small talk

title: At the Airport - Finding Your Gate
emotional_stakes: Your flight is boarding in 10 minutes and you can't find Gate 22!
ai_opening: Excuse me, you look a bit lost. Can I help you find something?
learning_goal: Practice asking for directions and understanding airport vocabulary

title: Visiting the Doctor

ai_opening: Good morning! I'm Dr. Smith. Please have a seat. What brings you in today?
learning_goal: Practice describing symptoms and understanding medical advice

title: Shopping for Clothes

ai_opening: Welcome! Is there anything specific you're looking for today?

title: Business Presentation
emotional_stakes: You're about to present your quarterly results to the board of directors.
ai_opening: Good morning everyone. Before we begin, I'd like to welcome our presenter. The floor is yours - please walk us through the quarterly results.
learning_goal: Practice formal business presentation language
rescue_hint: You could begin with: Thank you. I'd like to start by presenting...

title: Returning a Product
emotional_stakes: You bought a laptop yesterday and it's already broken. You're frustrated.
ai_opening: Hello, welcome to the customer service desk. How can I help you today?
learning_goal: Practice making complaints politely and negotiating returns
rescue_hint: You could say: I'd like to return this because...

title: Calling Your Landlord
emotional_stakes: The heating in your apartment broke during winter. It's freezing!
ai_opening: Hello? This is Mr. Johnson, your landlord. What can I do for you?
learning_goal: Practice reporting problems and requesting urgent help on the phone
rescue_hint: You could say: I'm calling because there's a problem with...

title: Morning Routine Talk
emotional_stakes: Your new coworker is curious about your daily life.
ai_opening: Hey! I'm always curious about how people start their mornings. What does your typical morning look like?
learning_goal: Practice describing daily routines using simple present tense
rescue_hint: You could say: I usually wake up at...

title: Debating Current Events
emotional_stakes: Your classmate disagrees with you and the debate is getting interesting.
ai_opening: I read an article about how artificial intelligence might replace many jobs in the next decade. What's your take on this?
learning_goal: Practice expressing opinions, agreeing, and disagreeing politely
rescue_hint: You could say: I think that... because...

title: Asking for Directions
emotional_stakes: You're lost in a new city and your phone battery just died.
ai_opening: Hi there! You seem a bit lost. Are you looking for something nearby?
learning_goal: Practice asking for and understanding directions
rescue_hint: You could say: Could you tell me how to get to...

title: Writing a Work Email Together
emotional_stakes: Your boss asked you to write an important email to a client, and you want to get it right.
ai_opening: So you need to write an email to the client about the project delay. Let's work on this together. First, how should we open the email?
learning_goal: Practice formal email writing and professional tone
rescue_hint: You could start with: Dear [name], I'm writing to inform you about...

title: Negotiating a Deal
emotional_stakes: This deal could save your company a lot of money. You need to negotiate well.
ai_opening: Thank you for meeting with us today. We've reviewed your proposal. While we're interested, we'd like to discuss the pricing structure. What flexibility do you have?
learning_goal: Practice business negotiation language and persuasive techniques
rescue_hint: You could say: We appreciate your interest. Let me suggest...

title: Understanding Idioms
emotional_stakes: Your American friend keeps using expressions you don't understand.
ai_opening: Man, this week has been crazy! I've been burning the candle at both ends. Anyway, what's been going on with you? Spill the beans!
suggested_vocab: burning the candle at both ends
learning_goal: Learn and practice common English idioms in natural conversation
rescue_hint: You could say: What does that expression mean?

title: At the Police Station
emotional_stakes: Someone stole your wallet with all your money and documents.

title: Cooking Together
